text.title: mocking-strategies Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.symptom: Tests break on every refactor that changes call signatures; many `assert_called_with(...)` assertions fail despite behavior being correct.
antipattern.root-cause: Treating mock as default rather than the special case where the call itself is the contract.
antipattern.fix: Default to stub for return values; reach for mock only when the API caller is observable behavior (e.g. cache hit count).

antipattern.description: Patching at the definition module: `mock.patch('module_b.function_b')` when module_a is the consumer.
antipattern.symptom: Test mocks the wrong reference; production code still calls the real function.
antipattern.root-cause: Confusion between where the name is defined vs where it is looked up.

antipattern.symptom: `await mocked_fn()` returns a Mock or raises TypeError; tests pass without exercising the awaited path.
antipattern.root-cause: Plain Mock doesn't return a coroutine.
antipattern.fix: Use AsyncMock from unittest.mock (Python ≥3.8). In vitest, use `vi.fn()` returning a Promise.

antipattern.description: Inline mock setup of 10+ lines in the test body.
antipattern.symptom: Test file is unreadable; intent buried under setup; mocks duplicated across tests.
antipattern.root-cause: No fixture extraction; either too many collaborators or wrong layering.
antipattern.fix: Extract a fixture (`@pytest.fixture` / `beforeEach`). If still bloated, reconsider the layering — too many collaborators in one unit.
